tool.name: MMseqs2 taxonomy
tool.description: of sequences by comparing them to a reference database
tool.command:
ln -s -f '${createdb.input_fasta}' 'input' && mmseqs createdb 'input' 'sequenceDB' --dbtype '$createdb.alph_type.dbtype' --shuffle $createdb.shuffle &&
##Used only for test #if str($download_tax_db) == 'true': cp -r '$database.database_type.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database* . &&
mmseqs createtaxdb database 'tmp' && #end if ##
#if $filtertaxseqdb.taxon_list mmseqs filtertaxseqdb ##Used only for test #if str($download_tax_db) == 'true': 'database' ## #else '$database.database_type.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database #end if 'database_filtered' --taxon-list '$filtertaxseqdb.taxon_list' && #end if
mmseqs taxonomy 'sequenceDB' #if $filtertaxseqdb.taxon_list 'database_filtered' #else ##Used only for test #if str($download_tax_db) == 'true': 'database' ## #else '$database.database_type.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database #end if #end if 'output_taxonomy' 'tmp' #if str($createdb.alph_type.dbtype) == "1" --comp-bias-corr-scale $createdb.alph_type.comp_bias_corr_scale #elif str($createdb.alph_type.dbtype) == "2" --zdrop $createdb.alph_type.zdrop #end if ##Pre-filter options --add-self-matches $taxonomy.prefilter.add_self_matches -s $taxonomy.prefilter.sensitivity -k $taxonomy.prefilter.kmer_length --target-search-mode $taxonomy.prefilter.target_search_mode ##--k-score TWIN k-mer threshold for generating similar k-mer lists [seq:2147483647,prof:2147483647] --max-seqs $taxonomy.prefilter.max_seqs --split $taxonomy.prefilter.split --split-mode $taxonomy.prefilter.split_mode ##--split-memory-limit BYTE Set max memory per split. E.g. 800B, 5K, 10M, 1G. Default (0) to all available system memory [0] --diag-score $taxonomy.prefilter.diag_score --exact-kmer-matching $taxonomy.prefilter.exact_kmer_matching --mask $taxonomy.prefilter.mask --mask-prob $taxonomy.prefilter.mask_prob --mask-lower-case $taxonomy.prefilter.mask_lower_case --mask-n-repeat $taxonomy.prefilter.mask_n_repeat --min-ungapped-score $taxonomy.prefilter.min_ungapped_score --spaced-kmer-mode $taxonomy.prefilter.spaced_kmer_mode ##--spaced-kmer-pattern STR User-specified spaced k-mer pattern [] ##--local-tmp STR Path where some of the temporary files will be created [] ##--disk-space-limit BYTE Set max disk space to use for reverse profile searches. E.g. 800B, 5K, 10M, 1G. Default (0) to all available disk space in the temp folder [0]
##Align options -a $taxonomy.align.convertalis ##The next 2 parameters seems to be the same --alignment-mode $taxonomy.align.alignment_mode --alignment-output-mode $taxonomy.align.alignment_output_mode --wrapped-scoring $taxonomy.align.wrapped_scoring -e $taxonomy.align.evalue --min-seq-id $taxonomy.align.min_seq_id --min-aln-len $taxonomy.align.min_aln_len --seq-id-mode $taxonomy.align.seq_id_mode --alt-ali $taxonomy.align.alt_ali -c $taxonomy.align.cov --cov-mode $taxonomy.align.cov_mode --max-rejected $taxonomy.align.max_rejected --max-accept $taxonomy.align.max_accept --score-bias $taxonomy.align.score_bias --realign $taxonomy.align.realign --realign-score-bias $taxonomy.align.realign_score_bias --realign-max-seqs $taxonomy.align.realign_max_seqs --corr-score-weight $taxonomy.align.corr_score_weight --exhaustive-search-filter $taxonomy.align.exhaustive_search_filter
##Profile options ##--pca Pseudo count admixture strength [] ##--pcb Pseudo counts: Neff at half of maximum admixture (range 0.0-inf) [] --mask-profile $taxonomy.profile.mask_profile --e-profile $taxonomy.profile.e_profile --wg $taxonomy.profile.wg --filter-msa $taxonomy.profile.filter_msa --filter-min-enable $taxonomy.profile.filter_min_enable --max-seq-id $taxonomy.profile.max_seq_id --qid $taxonomy.profile.qid --qsc $taxonomy.profile.qsc --cov $taxonomy.profile.cov --diff $taxonomy.profile.diff --pseudo-cnt-mode $taxonomy.profile.pseudo_cnt_mode --exhaustive-search $taxonomy.profile.exhaustive_search --lca-search $taxonomy.profile.lca_search
##Misc options ##--orf-filter INT Prefilter query ORFs with non-selective search ## Only used during nucleotide-vs-protein classification ## NOTE: Consider disabling when classifying short reads [1] --orf-filter-e $taxonomy.misc.orf_filter_e --orf-filter-s $taxonomy.misc.orf_filter_s --lca-mode $taxonomy.misc.lca_mode --majority $taxonomy.misc.majority --vote-mode $taxonomy.misc.vote_mode ##--lca-ranks STR Add column with specified ranks (',' separated) [] --tax-lineage $taxonomy.misc.tax_lineage #if str($taxonomy.misc.blacklist): --blacklist '$taxonomy.misc.blacklist' #end if #if str($taxonomy.misc.taxon_list): --taxon-list '$taxonomy.misc.taxon_list' #end if --rescore-mode $taxonomy.misc.rescore_mode --allow-deletion $taxonomy.misc.allow_deletion --min-length $taxonomy.misc.min_length --max-length $taxonomy.misc.max_length --max-gaps $taxonomy.misc.max_gaps --contig-start-mode $taxonomy.misc.contig_start_mode --contig-end-mode $taxonomy.misc.contig_end_mode --orf-start-mode $taxonomy.misc.orf_start_mode --forward-frames $taxonomy.misc.forward_frames --reverse-frames $taxonomy.misc.reverse_frames --translation-table $taxonomy.misc.translation_table --translate $taxonomy.misc.translate --use-all-table-starts $taxonomy.misc.use_all_table_starts --id-offset $taxonomy.misc.id_offset --sequence-overlap $taxonomy.misc.sequence_overlap --sequence-split-mode $taxonomy.misc.sequence_split_mode --headers-split-mode $taxonomy.misc.headers_split_mode --search-type $database.database_type.search_type --prefilter-mode $taxonomy.misc.prefilter_mode
##Common options ##--compressed INT Write compressed output [0] --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" ##-v INT Verbosity level: 0: quiet, 1: +errors, 2: +warnings, 3: +info [3] --max-seq-len $taxonomy.common.max_seq_len ##--db-load-mode INT Database preload mode 0: auto, 1: fread, 2: mmap, 3: mmap+touch [0] ##--mpi-runner STR Use MPI on compute cluster with this MPI command (e.g. "mpirun -np 42") [] ##--force-reuse BOOL Reuse tmp filse in tmp/latest folder ignoring parameters and version changes [0] ##--remove-tmp-files BOOL Delete temporary files [0]
##Expert options --filter-hits $taxonomy.expert.filter_hits --sort-results $taxonomy.expert.sort_results ##--create-lookup INT Create database lookup file (can be very large) [0] --chain-alignments $taxonomy.expert.chain_alignments --merge-query $taxonomy.expert.merge_query ##--strand INT Strand selection only works for DNA/DNA search 0: reverse, 1: forward, 2: both [1] && mmseqs createtsv 'sequenceDB' 'output_taxonomy' 'taxo_result.tsv'
--first-seq-as-repr $createtsv.first_seq_as_repr --target-column $createtsv.target_column --full-header $createtsv.full_header --idx-seq-src $createtsv.idx_seq_src --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" ##--compressed INT Write compressed output [0] ##-v INT Verbosity level: 0: quiet, 1: +errors, 2: +warnings, 3: +info [3] ##--db-output BOOL Return a result DB instead of a text file [0]
#if str($kraken_report.keep_report) == "Yes" && mmseqs taxonomyreport #if $filtertaxseqdb.taxon_list 'database_filtered' #else ##Used only for test #if str($download_tax_db) == 'true': 'database' ## #else '$database.database_type.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database #end if #end if 'output_taxonomy' 'taxo_result.txt' --report-mode 0 --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" #end if #if str($krona_report.keep_report) == "Yes" && mmseqs taxonomyreport #if $filtertaxseqdb.taxon_list 'database_filtered' #else ##Used only for test #if str($download_tax_db) == 'true': 'database' ## #else '$database.database_type.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database #end if #end if 'output_taxonomy' 'taxo_result.html' --report-mode 1 --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" #end if ##-v INT Verbosity level: 0: quiet, 1: +errors, 2: +warnings, 3: +info [3]

section.name: createdb
section.title: Convert FASTA/Q file(s) to MMseqs sequence DB format

param.name: input_fasta
param.label: Input fasta file
param.help: Protein or nucleotide sequences to classify taxonomically.

param.label: Shuffle input database
param.help: Randomize input order to improve load balancing during parallel processing.

section.name: database
section.title: Choose the taxonomic reference database that you want to use

param.name: type
param.label: Database type
param.help: Select a pre-indexed taxonomy database installed by the server administrator.

param.label: Taxonomy ID
param.help: Possibly multiple values separated by ','

section.name: taxonomy
section.title: Taxonomy assignment by computing the lowest common ancestor of homologs

param.label: Spaced k-mer mode
param.help: Spaced k-mers improve sensitivity for remote homologs. Consecutive mode is faster.

param.label: Accept only matches with ungapped alignment score above threshold
param.help: Prefilter candidates below this ungapped alignment score are discarded.

param.label: Target search mode
param.help: Similar k-mer mode finds more remote homologs but is slower.

param.label: Maximum results per query sequence allowed to pass the prefilter
param.help: Affects sensitivity

param.label: Split input into N equally distributed chunks
param.help: 0: set the best split automatically

param.label: Split mode
param.help: Controls how the database is split for parallel processing. Auto mode selects based on available memory.

param.label: Use ungapped diagonal scoring during prefilter
param.help: Speeds up prefiltering by scoring ungapped diagonals. Disable for very sensitive searches.

param.label: Extract only exact k-mers for matching
param.help: When enabled (1), only exact k-mer matches are used. Faster but less sensitive.

param.label: Alignment mode
param.help: Controls how much alignment detail is computed. Higher modes provide more information but are slower.

param.label: Maximum rejected alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped
param.help: Stop alignment calculation after this many consecutive rejected hits per query.

param.label: Maximum accepted alignments before alignment calculation for a query is stopped
param.help: Stop alignment calculation after this many accepted hits per query.

param.label: Filter result during search
param.help: Apply result filters during the search instead of as a post-processing step.

param.label: Rescore diagonals with
param.help: Method used to rescore candidate hits from the prefilter stage.
param.option: Longest alignment fulfilling window quality criterion

param.label: Numeric ids in index file are offset by this value
param.help: Add this offset to all numeric sequence IDs in the output.

param.label: Overlap between sequences
param.help: Number of residues that overlap when splitting long sequences into chunks.

param.label: Sequence split mode
param.help: Controls how long sequences are split into chunks for processing.
param.option: Soft link data and write new index

param.label: Headers split mode
param.help: Controls how headers are handled when sequences are split.

param.label: Prefilter mode
param.help: Selects the prefiltering strategy. Exhaustive mode disables prefiltering for maximum sensitivity.

param.label: Chain alignments
param.help: Chain consecutive alignments into a single longer alignment. Set 0 to disable.

param.label: Combine ORFs/split sequences to a single entry
param.help: Merge results from split query sequences or translated ORFs back into one entry per query.

section.name: createtsv
section.title: Create a tsv report from taxonomy output

param.name: keep_report
param.label: Do you want a Kraken style report
param.help: Kraken-style hierarchical taxonomy report with read counts at each taxonomic level.

param.name: keep_report
param.label: Do you want a Krona style report
param.help: Krona interactive HTML visualization of taxonomic composition.

has_line.line: // Krona is a flexible tool for exploring the relative proportions of
// Krona is a flexible tool for exploring the relative proportions of

tool.help:
@HELP_HEADER@
**About this tool**
This tool provides **fine-grained taxonomy assignment** by chaining individual MMseqs2 subcommands. It offers more control than **easy-taxonomy** (e.g., the ability to filter the reference database by taxon before searching), but requires a pre-built MMseqs2 taxonomy database on the server.
For most taxonomy workflows, **easy-taxonomy** is simpler and sufficient. Use this tool when you need to:
- Filter the reference database by specific taxa before searching (include/exclude taxon IDs) - Access advanced parameters for individual pipeline steps - Chain with other Galaxy tools between steps
**Pipeline steps**
This tool internally chains the following MMseqs2 subcommands:
1. **createdb** -- Convert query FASTA/FASTQ to MMseqs2 internal database format 2. **filtertaxseqdb** (optional) -- Filter the taxonomy database by a list of taxon IDs (e.g., include only Eukaryota, or exclude human sequences) 3. **taxonomy** -- Search queries against the reference and compute LCA of hits 4. **createtsv** -- Convert results to a human-readable tab-separated format 5. **taxonomyreport** (optional) -- Generate a Kraken-style or Krona-style hierarchical report
**Key parameters**
- **Sensitivity (-s)**: Controls search sensitivity (default 5.7). - **LCA mode (--lca-mode)**: Algorithm for computing the lowest common ancestor. - **Taxon filter (--taxon-list)**: Include or exclude specific NCBI taxon IDs from the reference database (e.g., ``2759`` for Eukaryota, ``!9606`` to exclude human). - **ORF extraction**: For nucleotide queries searched against protein databases, ORFs are extracted and translated automatically.
**Outputs**
- **Taxonomy TSV** (always produced): Tab-separated per-query taxonomy assignments - **Kraken report** (optional): Hierarchical report with read counts at each taxonomic level - **Krona report** (optional): Interactive HTML visualization of taxonomic composition
@HELP_FOOTER@
